stories.title: NBA Finals Media Day — Knicks and Spurs Speak Before Game 1 Tomorrow

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
MEDIA DAY is here. Game 1 is TOMORROW. 🏆

Knicks vs. Spurs — June 3, 8:30 PM ET on ABC.

NY: 11-game win streak, best point differential entering Finals in NBA history.
SA: Home court, Wemby, and a hungry city.

#NBAFinals #KnicksVsSpurs

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Knicks injury update: Mitchell Robinson will play Game 1 with a broken right pinkie.

Spurs: David Jones Garcia out for the season (ankle).

Tomorrow. 8:30 PM ET. ABC. Frost Bank Center.

This is it. #NBAFinals

facebook.long_form:
Tomorrow night, one of the most anticipated NBA Finals in decades tips off in San Antonio.

The New York Knicks and San Antonio Spurs gathered for Media Day today at the Frost Bank Center, the final stop before the games begin. Game 1 is Wednesday, June 3, at 8:30 PM ET on ABC.

What you need to know going in: The Knicks enter as arguably the hottest team in NBA postseason history. Their 11-game win streak has produced a +271 total point differential — the best ever by a team entering the NBA Finals. They swept the Cleveland Cavaliers in the Eastern Conference Finals. Jalen Brunson is the engine. Karl-Anthony Towns is the weapon. OG Anunoby is the lock.

The Spurs are 5.5-point home favorites. They earned this with a 62-20 regular season and survived a brutal seven-game Western Conference Finals against the defending champion Oklahoma City Thunder. Victor Wembanyama earned WCF MVP. His supporting cast of Stephon Castle and Dylan Harper are ready.

Injury note: Mitchell Robinson plans to play for the Knicks despite a broken right pinkie finger. Spurs' David Jones Garcia is out for the season with an ankle injury.

The series schedule: G1 June 3 / G2 June 5 (both in SA), G3 June 8 / G4 June 10 (both at MSG), G5 June 13 if needed. All games 8:30 PM ET on ABC.

The wait ends tomorrow. Are you ready? 🏆
facebook.image_caption:
Media Day done. Now it's time to play. 🏆

Game 1: Knicks vs. Spurs | Wednesday June 3 | 8:30 PM ET | ABC
Frost Bank Center, San Antonio

The Knicks haven't been here since 1999. The Spurs haven't been here since 2014. This one is going to be special.

stories.title: Wembanyama vs. Brunson — Finals MVP Race Heats Up as Odds Shift

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Finals MVP odds entering Game 1:

Wembanyama: -170 (favorite)
Brunson: +265

Wemby: 26.8/11.2/3.4 BLK regular season
Brunson: 28.4 PPG / 41.8% from three

Who wins it? #NBAFinals #Wembanyama #Brunson

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
The Spurs plan to throw Castle + Harper at Brunson before Wemby rotates.

Brunson averaged 27.4 PPG this postseason on 51.8% shooting.

Good luck with that. #NBAFinals #Knicks #Spurs

facebook.long_form:
The Finals MVP race starts before Game 1 tips off, and the market has made its call: Victor Wembanyama is the -170 favorite.

Wembanyama posted 26.8 points, 11.2 rebounds, 3.4 blocks, and 2.1 steals per game in the regular season. In the WCF against the defending champion Thunder, he averaged 27.3 points, 10.9 rebounds, and 2.7 blocks per game over seven games and earned WCF MVP.

Standing in his way is Jalen Brunson at +265. Brunson averaged 28.4 points and 6.1 assists per game in the regular season. Over his last 10 playoff games, he is averaging 27.4 points on 51.8 percent shooting. In three regular season meetings against the Spurs this year, he averaged 26 points, 4 rebounds, and 8 assists.

San Antonio plans to use Stephon Castle and Dylan Harper as the primary Brunson defenders before Wembanyama rotates. ESPN's First Take debated Monday which player faces greater pressure. The argument is compelling both ways. Either way, the basketball world wins.
facebook.image_caption:
Wembanyama -170. Brunson +265.

Two superstars. One trophy. One series.

Which one earns the Finals MVP? Let us know below. 👇 #NBAFinals

stories.title: Gregg Popovich's Shadow — The Legend Still Drives These Spurs

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Gregg Popovich is 77. He had a stroke in 2024. He uses a cane at practices.

He is still in the building. Still advising Mitch Johnson. Still making his Spurs.

That's why San Antonio is in the Finals. #Spurs #NBAFinals #Popovich

facebook.long_form:
Before Victor Wembanyama, before the 62-20 record and the Western Conference title — there was Gregg Popovich.

Pop is 77. In November 2024, he suffered a stroke that ended his coaching career. He has not stood on an NBA sideline in an official capacity since. But ask anyone in the Spurs organization, and they will tell you: he never really left.

Popovich now holds the title of President of Spurs Basketball. In practice, he is something closer to a coach emeritus. He attends practices, sometimes walking with the assistance of a cane. He works in the gym alongside Tim Duncan during rehab sessions. He watches film. He advises head coach Mitch Johnson.

Victor Wembanyama has spoken about Popovich with visible emotion. The relationship between the legend and the generational phenom is one of the defining threads of this franchise's current story.

The Spurs are in the NBA Finals because of what Popovich built — the culture, the system, the standard of professionalism. Mitch Johnson did not create those values. He inherited them, tended to them, and brought them to the championship round for the first time since 2014.

Gregg Popovich's shadow hangs over this Finals. It is the shadow of someone who built something worth watching.
facebook.image_caption:
He built this. 🙏

Gregg Popovich, 77, is still in the building — attending practices, advising the coaching staff, cheering on a team he poured his life into.

The Spurs are in the Finals. Pop helped make it possible.

article.headline: Gregg Popovich's Shadow: How the Spurs Legend Still Drives the 2026 Finals Run

stories.title: Steve Kerr Returns to Warriors on New 2-Year Contract

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
OFFICIAL: Steve Kerr is back at Golden State on a new 2-year deal.

Warriors went 37-45 and lost to the Suns in the play-in. This is Year 13 for Kerr.

Still the highest-paid coach in the NBA. #Warriors #NBA

facebook.long_form:
While the NBA Finals steal the spotlight, a significant piece of coaching news has landed: Steve Kerr is coming back to Golden State.

The Warriors and Kerr have agreed to a two-year contract, confirming his return for what will be his 13th season coaching the franchise. The deal keeps Kerr as the highest-paid coach in the NBA — he earned $17.5 million last season.

This comes after a difficult year. Golden State finished 37-45 and was eliminated by the Phoenix Suns in the play-in tournament. Kerr had announced in training camp he would not seek an extension, choosing instead to let the final year of his contract run out. After three weeks of deliberation — including meetings with owner Joe Lacob and GM Mike Dunleavy — Kerr signed.

The choice to come back says something. Kerr believes in this franchise. He believes there is more to accomplish. For Warriors fans who watched four championships under his leadership, that belief is worth something.
facebook.image_caption:
He's back. 🏆

Steve Kerr signs a new 2-year deal to remain as Golden State Warriors head coach.

Year 13 in the Bay. The work continues. #Warriors

stories.title: KAT and OG's Three-Point Barrage — The Hidden Weapon Against Wemby

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Spurs problem no one is talking about:

KAT is shooting 48.9% from three in these playoffs.
OG Anunoby is at 48.3%.

Wemby can't sag. Brunson attacks. Rinse, repeat.

That's the blueprint. #NBAFinals #Knicks

facebook.long_form:
Victor Wembanyama is the best defensive player on the planet. But the New York Knicks have built something that creates a genuine problem for him — and it starts from behind the arc.

Karl-Anthony Towns is shooting 48.9 percent from three in these 2026 playoffs. That is a career playoff best. OG Anunoby is at 48.3 percent from three. When two of the five players on the floor are converting at that clip, something has to give.

Wembanyama's defensive system is built around protecting the paint. But that system has a flaw against a lineup that includes both KAT and OG as genuine long-range threats. If he sags to protect the paint, one of them buries a three. If he closes out, Brunson has an open lane. There is no correct answer.

Anunoby also provides a defensive answer on the other end. Per efficiency metrics, he has allowed the fewest points per 100 matchup possessions as a primary Wembanyama defender of any player who has guarded him more than 100 half-court possessions this season.

The Knicks have an offense that exploits Wembanyama's scheme and a defender who can neutralize him on the other end. Whether it works over seven games is the question Game 1 begins to answer.
facebook.image_caption:
KAT: 48.9% from three in the 2026 playoffs.
OG Anunoby: 48.3%.

Wembanyama's defensive system doesn't have an answer for this. Tomorrow night, we find out how the Spurs adjust. #NBAFinals #Knicks

article.headline: KAT and OG's Three-Point Barrage: The Knicks' Hidden Weapon Against Wembanyama

KAT and OG Three-Point Efficiency
**Source:** ESPN, FanDuel Research, Yahoo Sports, The Ringer
**Key Facts:**
- KAT 2026 postseason 3P%: 48.9% (career playoff best)
- OG Anunoby 2026 postseason 3P%: 48.3%
- Five-out lineup with KAT at center removes Wemby from preferred paint position
- Wemby zone-adjacent system ineffective vs. KAT + OG as perimeter threats
- Creates open driving lanes for Brunson when Wemby forced to close out
- OG Anunoby: fewest points per 100 matchups as primary Wemby defender (most possessions in league)
- Confidence: HIGH (multiple sources)
